tools.name: presentation-design-prompts
tools.oneLiner: AI chat tools write decent slide content.
tools.description:
AI chat tools write decent slide content. Design is where they fall apart: clip-art, clashing colors, bullet walls. A sharper prompt fixes that.
Each theme here is one prompt that carries a full design system: a color palette pinned to hex values, named Google Fonts, a layout grammar, and an avoid-list that stops the model from decorating. Paste it into ChatGPT or Claude, say what your talk covers

tools.description:
An external memory for LLMs — a bi-temporal knowledge graph with hybrid (keyword + vector) retrieval and a self-improving reflection loop.

tools.name: floe-guard
tools.oneLiner: A local budget guardrail for AI agents.
tools.description:
A local budget guardrail for AI agents. It hard-stops your agent before its next LLM call when it would cross a spend ceiling — so a runaway loop dies at $0.10 instead of $4,000. No account, no signup, no network. Runs in your process.
pip install floe-guard
from floe_guard import BudgetGuard
guard = BudgetGuard(limit_usd=5.00) # your ceiling
guard.check() # before each LLM call — raises if it'd c

tools.name: One Model Won't Save You
tools.oneLiner: I was grabbing coffee with a peer last week who was tearing his hair out trying to find the “perfect” AI platform for his entire company.
tools.description:
Pedro Rocha
CTO of xelerate.tech
Porto, 4th of May 2026
One Model Won’t Save You: How We Actually Built Our AI Stack
I was grabbing coffee with a peer last week who was tearing his hair out trying to find the “perfect” AI platform for his entire company. He wanted one contract, one interface, and one bill.
I told him he was chasing a ghost.
The “one-size-fits-all” AI platform is a marketing myth.
